input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

recent_thinks: [0-2] A person in a plaid shirt and tan pants pushes a long, green-painted wooden beam with a rounded edge across a jointer table. The beam advances slowly over the metal bed. Large black text reading "DAVID CAN MAKE THAT" is overlaid at the bottom. Workshop tools and boxes are visible in the blurred background on the concrete floor.

[2-4] The large black text overlay reading "DAVID CAN MAKE THAT" vanishes from the bottom of the frame. The individual in the plaid shirt continues sliding the long, green-painted wooden beam with a rounded edge forward across the shiny metal jointer bed. Their gloved hands maintain a steady grip on the wood as it advances.

[4-6] A close-up shot shows hands cutting a strip of white paper with black scissors. Below the paper lies a yellow rectangular sharpening stone with a dark metal surface. The background is a clean white table, marking a complete scene change from the earlier woodworking environment.

samples.output: <think>The hands finish cutting a strip from the white paper using black scissors. Once separated, the hands hold and manipulate the thin piece of paper above the white table surface. The yellow rectangular sharpening stone with a dark metal insert remains stationary in the foreground throughout the sequence.</think><action>silent</action>

[18-22] Black scissors with a red pivot point cut a strip of the white paper while the yellow tool rests on the white table surface. [70-74] A hand places a yellow and green wrapped tool on the upper metal table and points a finger at the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, highlighting a wet sheen. The red and black hammer handle remains fixed on the right side as the tool polishes the surface.</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range": [70, 86], "text": "[70-74] A hand places a yellow and green wrapped tool on the upper metal table and points a finger at the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, highlighting a wet sheen. [74-80] A gloved hand with a white fabric back, black leather palm, and plaid sleeve enters to press and wipe the lower metal surface where liquid was applied. [80-86] A bare hand then takes the yellow and green wrapped tool and rubs it back and forth along the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, polishing the surface while a red and black hammer handle remains visible on the right.", "parse_success": true}</summary>

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A person in a plaid shirt and tan pants pushes a long, green-painted wooden beam with a rounded edge across a jointer table. Large black text reading "DAVID CAN MAKE THAT" is overlaid at the bottom before vanishing. [4-8] The scene changes to a white table where hands cut a strip of white paper with black scissors above a yellow rectangular sharpening stone with a dark metal surface. [8-14] Hands manipulate the cut paper to wipe the stone, then wrap the strip around the yellow plastic casing, completely encasing the tool into a white rectangular block with only the yellow end visible. [14-18] Hands smooth white paper around a yellow rectangular tool, adjusting the wrapping to fully encase the casing. [18-22] Black scissors with a red pivot point cut a strip of the white paper while the yellow tool rests on the white table surface. [22-28] A roll of bright green tape is introduced; a piece is torn and pressed onto the white paper wrapping to secure it around the yellow object, with the scissors resting stationary on the left.

[28-38] Hands press and smooth bright green tape onto white paper wrapping surrounding a yellow rectangular tool. Fingers secure the adhesive firmly around the casing. Black scissors with a red pivot remain stationary on the left white table surface. [38-40] Hands lift and rotate the wrapped tool, revealing a metal blade extending from the yellow plastic casing, secured by the green tape. [40-42] The scene shifts to an extreme close-up of the metal blade resting in a slot on a dark machine table. The yellow handle is blurred in the background, and a finger hovers near the blade's edge as the camera focuses on the metal surface.

[42-50] In an extreme close-up, a metal blade rests securely in a jointer cutter head slot while a finger presses a yellow-handled tool against it. The yellow plastic end is pushed forward and aligned flush against the blade's side to stabilize or check alignment. [50-56] The camera shifts to a side view showing the white-wrapped tool resting on the flat metal table adjacent to the slot. A hand holding a white bottle with a red label dispenses a drop of clear liquid onto the table surface near the cutter head, while a hammer with a red and black handle rests on the machine's right edge.

[56-58] A hand holding a white bottle with a red label withdraws after placing a drop of clear liquid on the metal table, where a paper-wrapped tool with a yellow end sits in the background and a hammer with a red and black handle rests on the right edge. [58-70] A hand enters to grasp the white and green paper-wrapped tool with the yellow plastic end, sliding it back and forth along the metal table surface near the cutter head slot. This rubbing motion spreads the clear liquid to polish the flat metal bed, while the hammer remains stationary on the right edge.

[70-74] A hand places a yellow and green wrapped tool on the upper metal table and points a finger at the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, highlighting a wet sheen. [74-80] A gloved hand with a white fabric back, black leather palm, and plaid sleeve enters to press and wipe the lower metal surface where liquid was applied. [80-86] A bare hand then takes the yellow and green wrapped tool and rubs it back and forth along the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, polishing the surface while a red and black hammer handle remains visible on the right.

recent_thinks: [86-88] The bare hand maintains its grip on the yellow and green wrapped tool, continuing the back-and-forth rubbing motion along the metal table near the cutter head gap. The red and black hammer handle remains fixed on the right side as the tool polishes the surface.

[88-90] A bare hand continues rubbing the yellow and green wrapped tool back and forth across the metal table surface near the cutter head gap. The tool is pressed firmly against the metal as the hand moves it in a polishing motion, while a red and black hammer handle rests stationary on the machine's right edge.

queries.question: Which activity is performed first in the video sequence? A. Cutting white paper with black handled scissors B. Feeding lumber through a woodworking machine C. Manipulating a roll of bright green tape D. Peeling paper wrapping off a white cylinder

samples.output: <think>The bare hand continues to slide the yellow and green wrapped tool along the metal table surface near the cutter head gap. The tool maintains contact with the metal as the hand moves it in a polishing motion, while a red and black hammer handle rests stationary on the machine's right edge.</think><action>silent</action>

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A person in a plaid shirt and tan pants pushes a long, green-painted wooden beam with a rounded edge across a jointer table. [4-8] The scene changes to a white table where hands cut a strip of white paper with black scissors above a yellow rectangular sharpening stone with a dark metal surface. [8-14] Hands manipulate the cut paper to wipe the stone, then wrap the strip around the yellow plastic casing, completely encasing the tool into a white rectangular block with only the yellow end visible.

[14-18] Hands smooth white paper around a yellow rectangular tool, adjusting the wrapping to fully encase the casing. [18-22] Black scissors with a red pivot point cut a strip of the white paper while the yellow tool rests on the white table surface. [22-28] A roll of bright green tape is introduced; a piece is torn and pressed onto the white paper wrapping to secure it around the yellow object, with the scissors resting stationary on the left.

[28-38] Hands press and smooth bright green tape onto white paper wrapping surrounding a yellow rectangular tool. Fingers secure the adhesive firmly around the casing. Black scissors with a red pivot remain stationary on the left white table surface. [38-40] Hands lift and rotate the wrapped tool, revealing a metal blade extending from the yellow plastic casing, secured by the green tape. [40-42] The scene shifts to an extreme close-up of the metal blade resting in a slot on a dark machine table. The yellow handle is blurred in the background, and a finger hovers near the blade's edge as the camera focuses on the metal surface.

[42-50] In an extreme close-up, a metal blade rests securely in a jointer cutter head slot while a finger presses a yellow-handled tool against it. The yellow plastic end is pushed forward and aligned flush against the blade's side to stabilize or check alignment. [50-56] The camera shifts to a side view showing the white-wrapped tool resting on the flat metal table adjacent to the slot. A hand holding a white bottle with a red label dispenses a drop of clear liquid onto the table surface near the cutter head, while a hammer with a red and black handle rests on the machine's right edge.

[56-58] A hand holding a white bottle with a red label withdraws after placing a drop of clear liquid on the metal table, where a paper-wrapped tool with a yellow end sits in the background and a hammer with a red and black handle rests on the right edge. [58-70] A hand enters to grasp the white and green paper-wrapped tool with the yellow plastic end, sliding it back and forth along the metal table surface near the cutter head slot. This rubbing motion spreads the clear liquid to polish the flat metal bed, while the hammer remains stationary on the right edge.

[4-8] The scene changes to a white table where hands cut a strip of white paper with black scissors above a yellow rectangular sharpening stone with a dark metal surface. [8-14] Hands manipulate the cut paper to wipe the stone, then wrap the strip around the yellow plastic casing, completely encasing the tool into a white rectangular block with only the yellow end visible. [14-18] Hands smooth white paper around a yellow rectangular tool, adjusting the wrapping to fully encase the casing. [18-22] Black scissors with a red pivot point cut a strip of the white paper while the yellow tool rests on the white table surface. [22-28] A roll of bright green tape is introduced; a piece is torn and pressed onto the white paper wrapping to secure it around the yellow object, with the scissors resting stationary on the left.

[28-38] Hands press and smooth bright green tape onto white paper wrapping surrounding a yellow rectangular tool. Fingers secure the adhesive firmly around the casing. Black scissors with a red pivot remain stationary on the left white table surface. [38-40] Hands lift and rotate the wrapped tool, revealing a metal blade extending from the yellow plastic casing, secured by the green tape. [40-42] The scene shifts to an extreme close-up of the metal blade resting in a slot on a dark machine table. The yellow handle is blurred in the background, and a finger hovers near the blade's edge as the camera focuses on the metal surface.

[42-50] In an extreme close-up, a metal blade rests securely in a jointer cutter head slot while a finger presses a yellow-handled tool against it. The yellow plastic end is pushed forward and aligned flush against the blade's side to stabilize or check alignment. [50-56] The camera shifts to a side view showing the white-wrapped tool resting on the flat metal table adjacent to the slot. A hand holding a white bottle with a red label dispenses a drop of clear liquid onto the table surface near the cutter head, while a hammer with a red and black handle rests on the machine's right edge.

[56-58] A hand holding a white bottle with a red label withdraws after placing a drop of clear liquid on the metal table, where a paper-wrapped tool with a yellow end sits in the background and a hammer with a red and black handle rests on the right edge. [58-70] A hand enters to grasp the white and green paper-wrapped tool with the yellow plastic end, sliding it back and forth along the metal table surface near the cutter head slot. This rubbing motion spreads the clear liquid to polish the flat metal bed, while the hammer remains stationary on the right edge.

[70-74] A hand places a yellow and green wrapped tool on the upper metal table and points a finger at the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, highlighting a wet sheen. [74-80] A gloved hand with a white fabric back, black leather palm, and plaid sleeve enters to press and wipe the lower metal surface where liquid was applied. [80-86] A bare hand then takes the yellow and green wrapped tool and rubs it back and forth along the lower metal table near the cutter head slot, polishing the surface while a red and black hammer handle remains visible on the right.

recent_thinks: [86-88] The bare hand maintains its grip on the yellow and green wrapped tool, continuing the back-and-forth rubbing motion along the metal table near the cutter head gap. The red and black hammer handle remains fixed on the right side as the tool polishes the surface.

[88-90] A bare hand continues rubbing the yellow and green wrapped tool back and forth across the metal table surface near the cutter head gap. The tool is pressed firmly against the metal as the hand moves it in a polishing motion, while a red and black hammer handle rests stationary on the machine's right edge.

[90-92] The bare hand continues to slide the yellow and green wrapped tool along the metal table surface near the cutter head gap. The tool maintains contact with the metal as the hand moves it in a polishing motion, while a red and black hammer handle rests stationary on the machine's right edge.

[92-94] A gloved hand with a black leather palm and white fabric back presses against the metal table surface near the cutter head gap. The fingers are curled as the hand wipes or inspects the polished metal area, where bright light reflects off the smooth surface.

[94-96] The gloved hand with a black leather palm and white fabric back continues to press against the metal surface near the cutter head gap. Fingers remain curled as the hand wipes the polished area, where a bright reflection of light shines intensely off the smooth, flat metal table surface.

[96-98] The gloved hand with a black leather palm remains pressed against the metal table surface adjacent to the cutter head gap. Fingers stay curled as the hand wipes the polished section, highlighted by a bright light reflection shining off the smooth, flat metal bed.

queries.question: Are the black scissors with a red pivot screw, previously seen on the white surface during the crafting activity, still present in the scene with the metal rod and vise?

samples.output: <think>A gloved hand with a black leather palm continues to press against the metal table surface near the cutter head gap. The fingers remain curled as the hand inspects or wipes the polished area, where a bright light reflection shines off the smooth, flat metal bed.</think><action>silent</action>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
